sections.paragraph: Ch. i.q. אֲזַל to go away, to depart, (comp. δάκρυον, lacrima; الاس, ἀδάμας). Hence Dan 2:5, 8, מִלְּתָא מִנִּי אַזְדָּא, “the word has gone out from me,” i.e. what I have said is ratified, and will not be recalled; comp. 9:23 Isa 45:23. The Hebrew interpreters, as Saad. Tanch. of Jerusalem, have long ago rightly compared the Talmudic phrase אזדא לטעמיה “to go to one’s opinion,” i.e. to follow one’s own opinion. As to the form, אַזְרָּא is part. fem. from the masc. אֲזֵר (of the form אֲזֵל, קְטֵל).
